selected_important_turn.text: funnny you but no. so what else will i be left with if i give you those. okay let say i get 2 food, 2 firewood and 1 water.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the first concrete, competitive counter-offer after the opponent's aggressive demand. It sets a zero-sum tone and anchors the negotiation into positional bargaining (both sides trading firm, self-oriented offers). That early anchoring shaped subsequent rounds and escalated inference of competing priorities.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The reply is bluntly dismissive and immediately re-asserts a self-centered allocation without exploring the opponent's reasons or creating tradeoffs. It misses the chance to probe priorities (what matters most to them) or propose package trades that could create value (e.g., tradeoffs across items or small concessions for mutual benefit). Its dismissive tone also reduces rapport.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Replace the blunt refusal with a short, empathetic acknowledgement of their needs plus one or two integrative moves: ask which item is most important to them, state your top priorities briefly, and propose a specific trade that gives them what they value most while compensating with a concession on a less-important item.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: likely increase (opening tradeoffs could reveal opportunities for mutually better splits)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: improve (more balanced bargaining and information-sharing reduces arbitrary anchoring)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (less positional rigidity makes agreement easier)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve (less confrontational tone and more curiosity build trust)

value2reason.Low: i will need it when i go for hiking
value2reason.Medium: i will need it for emergency at night.
value2reason.High: i will need it for the long trip.

value2reason.Low: I like to hike and I will bring along my 2 dogs so extra water is needed for my puppies.
value2reason.Medium: I need to cook the food and keep warm at night.  I get cold very easily so the warmth of firewood would be really important.  I didn't spend a lot on a sleeping bag.
value2reason.High: I am a terrible cook so I need as much food as possible so I can prepare as best as I can as some of my cooking will be bad.  So I'd like all the food.

dialogue.text: So far so good.  What are your plans for your camping trip?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: yes i guess you too. i will need more supplies like 3 foods, 2 firewood and1 water
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I have not really camped before so I am looking for as many supplies as possible.  What about I get 3 food, 3 firewood, and 1 water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: funnny you but no. so what else will i be left with if i give you those. okay let say i get 2 food, 2 firewood and 1 water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need all the food for my cooking, and I’d like 3 firewood for warmth. You can have the remaining water. Let’s split it that way.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I understand you need food for cooking and firewood for warmth. My top priority is food too, but I also need firewood for emergencies. How about we split the food 2-1 and firewood 2-1, then share the water equally? That way we both get what we need.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need all the food for cooking and 3 firewood for warmth. You can have the remaining water. Let’s agree on this split.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
